Title: Transcription Factor Amr1 Induces Melanin Biosynthesis and Suppresses Virulence in Alternaria brassicicola

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Protection from UV irradiation and enzymatic lysis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced in wild type at late infection when Amr1 increases; 8- to 30-fold lower in Δamr1 mutants
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Lower expression in Δamr1 is associated with melanin deficiency
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Brn2 is a downstream melanin-pathway gene positively regulated by Amr1. 2. Its transcript abundance is strongly reduced in Δamr1 mutants. 3. Reduced Brn2 expression correlates with loss of melanin accumulation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not directly genetically manipulated in this paper

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Protection from UV irradiation and enzymatic lysis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly induced in wild type at late infection; 8- to 30-fold lower in Δamr1 mutants
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced expression in Δamr1 is associated with melanin deficiency
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Scd1 is a melanin biosynthetic structural gene activated by Amr1. 2. Its expression is strongly decreased in Δamr1 mutants. 3. This supports Amr1-dependent activation of the melanin pathway.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Protection from UV irradiation and enzymatic lysis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly induced in wild type at late infection; 8- to 30-fold lower in Δamr1 mutants
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced expression in Δamr1 is associated with melanin deficiency
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Brn1 is a downstream melanin-pathway structural gene under positive Amr1 control. 2. Its transcript level is greatly reduced in Δamr1 mutants. 3. This reduction is consistent with the melanin-deficient phenotype.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Uses acetyl-CoA or malonyl-CoA precursors to synthesize the melanin intermediate T4HN
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Protects fungal cells from UV irradiation and enzymatic lysis; contributes to environmental stress tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Conidial primary cell walls and septa
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated about four-fold in Δamr1 mutants during late infection; associated with melanizing tissues
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Late-stage infection on Brassica oleracea; PDA culture
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene disruption; mutant phenotype analysis; pathogenicity assay; expression profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Disruption causes melanin-deficient conidia but does not alter virulence on host plants
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AbPks7 encodes a melanin-associated polyketide synthase in A. brassicicola. 2. Its mutants are melanin deficient, confirming a role in 1,8-DHN melanin biosynthesis. 3. abpks7 mutants retain wild-type virulence, showing that melanin deficiency alone does not explain the hypervirulence of Δamr1 mutants.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Melanin deficiency may increase susceptibility to environmental stress, though virulence was unchanged in this study
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Annotated as three separate genes in the genome assembly according to the paper; used mainly to test whether melanin itself affects virulence

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: C2H2 zinc finger motifs; fungal-specific Zn(II)2Cys6 binuclear motif
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Melanin biosynthesis and repression of hydrolytic enzyme-associated nutrient acquisition during late pathogenesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of melanin biosynthesis genes and transcriptional repression of a subset of hydrolytic enzyme genes
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Brn2; Scd1; Brn1; AbPks7; Cbh7; lipase; chymotrypsin; multiple glycoside hydrolases; pectate lyases; pectin esterase; CutAb
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates Brn2, Scd1, Brn1 and AbPks7-associated melanin pathway genes; represses many hydrolytic enzyme-coding genes during late infection
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Induced during late-stage infection/conidiogenesis; expression low during early infection
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Possible absence of feedback inhibition inferred from elevated GFP transcript in promoter-tagged mutant; not definitively established
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in highly melanized conidia and aerial hyphae; induced during late infection (around 88 hpi) but low during early infection
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly melanized conidia and late infecting hyphae
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Late-stage infection on green cabbage; conidiogenesis; PDA growth; pectin as carbon source
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Protection from UV irradiation and glucanase digestion; environmental survival
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Targeted disruption and deletion mutants; complementation with native and constitutive constructs; GFP localization; RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; pathogenicity assays; growth assays on pectin
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes melanin biosynthesis but suppresses virulence; deletion causes melanin deficiency, pink pigment secretion, enhanced pectin utilization, higher hydrolytic enzyme expression, UV/glucanase sensitivity, and larger lesions
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Amr1 is a Cmr1 homolog and nuclear transcription factor required for normal melanin biosynthesis in A. brassicicola. 2. Δamr1 mutants show sharply reduced expression of Brn2, Scd1, Brn1 and reduced AbPks7 expression, causing melanin-deficient conidia and pink pigment secretion. 3. Δamr1 mutants are hypervirulent, require fewer conidia for successful infection, and grow better on pectin. 4. RNA-seq showed Amr1 negatively regulates many hydrolytic enzyme genes during late infection while positively regulating melanin-pathway genes. 5. Complementation restores melanin and wild-type virulence, confirming causality.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key positive regulator of melanin biosynthesis and condition-dependent suppressor of virulence-associated nutrient acquisition genes
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of Amr1 increases virulence but causes melanin deficiency, UV sensitivity, and glucanase sensitivity, indicating a trade-off between pathogenic aggressiveness and environmental survival
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mutation uniquely increases virulence unlike most known pathogenicity genes in this pathosystem; suggested to support adaptation as saprophyte/facultative parasite
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq during infection (GEO: GSE36781); qRT-PCR validation
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native Amr1 promoter; TrpC constitutive promoter

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Pathogenesis-associated regulation; specific nutrient metabolism role not established
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene disruption; gene deletion; complementation; pathogenicity assays; Southern blot verification
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion reduces lesion diameter by about 55%; required for full virulence
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AbVf8 is a novel pathogenesis-associated putative transcription factor with a predicted SET domain. 2. Deletion mutants show markedly reduced virulence but normal vegetative growth and conidiation. 3. The study did not define a direct nutrient-metabolism metabolite controlled by AbVf8.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Not established for a nutrient metabolite
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No major growth or conidiation defect reported despite virulence reduction
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although a core experimental gene, no explicit nutrient-metabolism causal link was demonstrated in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Predicted SET domain suggests possible chromatin/epigenetic regulation, but no direct evidence was provided
